addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. - anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. - artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. - verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). - schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. input.prompt: Extract the full content of the challenge description, especially "Parte 2 - Sistema de sincronización" / "Part 2 - Sync system". What exactly needs to be built? List all requirements, deliverables (NOTES.md, POC sync), dev containers requirements, and any details about the sync system.
Extract the full API documentation. What endpoints, data models, authentication, and operations does this MCP/API expose? Describe the Todo lists and items API in detail.

content.content:
# Crunchloop Senior Engineer Challenge - Summary

## Overview
This is a post-interview technical challenge focused on building API integration and data synchronization capabilities for a Todo API system.

## Core Requirements

### Primary Objectives
1. **Synchronization Logic**: Create a mechanism to sync `TodoLists` and `TodoItems` between a local API and an external API, supporting create, update, and delete operations
2. **Resilience**: Implement partial failure handling and retry mechanisms
3. **Performance**: Minimize external API calls
4. **Error Handling**: Provide meaningful error messages and detailed debugging logs
5. **Documentation**: Document design decisions in a `NOTES.md` file

### Key Technical Tasks
- Develop sync functionality that detects changes in external API and propagates local modifications
- Handle data synchronization gracefully, including deletion scenarios
- Optimize to reduce unnecessary API interactions, especially for large datasets

## Deliverables

1. **Source Code**
   - Synchronization implementation
   - Test cases demonstrating correctness

2. **Documentation (`NOTES.md`)**
   - High-level architectural overview
   - Design decisions and trade-offs
   - Resilience and error handling strategies
   - Edge case analysis
   - Improvement recommendations
   - Implementation assumptions

3. **Instructions**
   - Steps to execute the solution and run synchronization

## Evaluation Criteria
- Functional correctness and edge case handling
- Code quality, modularity, and maintainability
- Performance efficiency
- Clear documentation of reasoning

**Note**: The external API documentation is referenced but not fully provided in the content above.

content.content:
# External Todo API Documentation

## Overview
This is a Todo management API with endpoints for organizing tasks into lists and individual items. The base URL is `http://localhost`.

## API Endpoints

### TodoList Operations
- **Create**: `POST /todolists` - Establish a new list with initial items
- **Retrieve**: `GET /todolists` - Fetch all lists and their associated items
- **Update**: `PATCH /todolists/{todolistId}` - Modify an existing list
- **Delete**: `DELETE /todolists/{todolistId}` - Remove a list and all its items

### TodoItem Operations
- **Update**: `PATCH /todolists/{todolistId}/todoitems/{todoitemId}` - Modify a specific item
- **Delete**: `DELETE /todolists/{todolistId}/todoitems/{todoitemId}` - Remove an individual item

## Data Models

The API uses six models:
1. **TodoList** - Represents a todo list container
2. **TodoItem** - Represents individual tasks within a list
3. **CreateTodoListBody** - Request payload for list creation
4. **CreateTodoItemBody** - Request payload for item creation
5. **UpdateTodoListBody** - Request payload for list modifications
6. **UpdateTodoItemBody** - Request payload for item modifications

## Authentication
"All endpoints do not require authorization," making this an open API without authentication barriers.

## Key Design
The API uses nested resource routing, organizing items hierarchically under parent lists, enabling logical task management at both collection and individual levels.

content.content:
# Summary of Crunchloop Senior Engineer Challenge

## Overview
The challenge involves building a synchronization mechanism for a Todo API to enable seamless data sharing between a local instance and an external API system.

## Core Requirements

The submission must address these areas:

**Synchronization Functionality**
- Create bidirectional sync for TodoLists and TodoItems
- Handle creation of new items detected in external systems
- Propagate local modifications outward
- Manage deletions appropriately

**System Resilience**
The solution should "ensure the synchronization process handles partial failures" and "implement retry mechanisms."

**Optimization**
Developers must "minimize API calls to the external API as much as possible."

**Documentation & Logging**
- Provide meaningful error messages
- Include detailed logs for debugging
- Create a NOTES.md file explaining design rationale

## Deliverables Expected

1. Source code with synchronization logic and test cases
2. NOTES.md documenting design decisions and trade-offs
3. Instructions for running the solution

## NOTES.md Requirements

The documentation should include:
- High-level approach summary
- Design decision rationale
- Resilience and error handling strategies
- Edge case identification
- Improvement recommendations
- Underlying assumptions

## Important Note

The prompt references external API documentation at "./docs/README.md" but that file was not provided in the content shared. Implementation details about the external API endpoint, specific schema requirements, and how to run the external API service are not included in this excerpt.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

---

## Rails Project Exploration Report

### 1. **TodoList Model**
**File:** `/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/app/models/todo_list.rb`

**Fields & Validations:**
- `name` (string, null: false) — required presence validation
- `id` (primary key, auto-generated)
- Created via `create_table` (no timestamps visible in schema, though schema shows version date)

**Associations:**
- `has_many :todo_items, dependent: :destroy` — cascading delete

**Validations:**
- `validates :name, presence: true`

---

### 2. **TodoItem Model**
**File:** `/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/app/models/todo_item.rb`

**Fields & Types:**
- `title` (string, null: false)
- `complete` (boolean, default: false, null: false)
- `todo_list_id` (integer, null: false, foreign key)
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (timestamps)

**Associations:**
- `belongs_to :todo_list`

**Validations:**
- `validates :title, presence: true`
- `validates :complete, inclusion: { in: [true, false] }`

---

### 3. **Database Schema**
**File:** `/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/db/schema.rb`

**todo_lists table:**
```ruby
create_table "todo_lists", force: :cascade do |t|
  t.string "name", null: false
end
```

**todo_items table:**
```ruby
create_table "todo_items", force: :cascade do |t|
  t.string "title", null: false
  t.boolean "complete", default: false, null: false
  t.integer "todo_list_id", null: false
  t.datetime "created_at", null: false
  t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
  t.index ["todo_list_id"], name: "index_todo_items_on_todo_list_id"
end

add_foreign_key "todo_items", "todo_lists"
```

**Schema version:** 2026_06_25_234734

---

### 4. **Controllers & Actions**

**TodoListsController** (`/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`)
- `index` — GET /todolists
- `show` — GET /todolists/:id
- `new` — GET /todolists/new
- `edit` — GET /todolists/:id/edit
- `create` — POST /todolists (supports Turbo Stream & HTML)
- `update` — PATCH /todolists/:id (supports Turbo Stream & HTML)
- `destroy` — DELETE /todolists/:id (supports Turbo Stream & HTML)
- Uses `respond_to` for format negotiation

**TodoItemsController** (`/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`)
- `index` — GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
- `show` — GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
- `new` — GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
- `edit` — GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
- `create` — POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems (supports Turbo Stream & HTML)
- `update` — PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id (supports Turbo Stream & HTML)
- `destroy` — DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id (supports Turbo Stream & HTML)
- `check_all` — PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/check_all (custom collection action, marks all items complete)

**Api::TodoListsController** (`/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/app/controllers/api/todo_lists_controller.rb`)
- `index` — GET /api/todolists (JSON only, raises `ActionController::RoutingError` for HTML)

---

### 5. **Routes**
**File:** `/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/config/routes.rb`

```ruby
namespace :api do
  resources :todo_lists, only: %i[index], path: :todolists
end

resources :todo_lists, path: :todolists do
  resources :todo_items, path: :todoitems do
    patch :check_all, on: :collection
  end
end

root "todo_lists#index"
```

**URL Patterns:**
- API: `GET /api/todolists` (JSON)
- Web: `GET /todolists`, `POST /todolists`, `PATCH /todolists/:id`, `DELETE /todolists/:id`
- Nested items: `GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems`, `POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems`, etc.
- Custom: `PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/check_all`

---

### 6. **Gemfile**
**File:** `/Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/Gemfile`

**Key Gems:**
- **Rails:** `~> 7.0.4`, `>= 7.0.4.3`
- **Database:** `sqlite3 ~> 1.6`
- **Web Server:** `puma ~> 5.0`
- **Frontend:**
  - `turbo-rails` (Turbo Drive & Streams)
  - `stimulus-rails` (Stimulus JS)
  - `tailwindcss-rails`
  - `importmap-rails`
- **JSON API:** `jbuilder` (no Active Model Serializers)
- **Testing (dev/test groups):**
  - `rspec-rails` (development)
  - `rspec` (test)
  - `capybara` (integration tests)
  - `selenium-webdriver` (system tests)
  - `webdrivers` (driver management)

**Missing Gems:** No Sidekiq, Faraday, HTTParty, or Active Model Serializers

---

### 7. **API Endpoints & Serializers**

**JSON Templates (jbuilder):**
- `/app/views/api/todo_lists/index.json.jbuilder` — returns array of `{id, name}`

content.content:
# OpenAPI Todo API Specification Summary

## Paths & HTTP Methods

- **GET /todolists** – Retrieve all TodoLists with items
- **POST /todolists** – Create new TodoList with optional items
- **PATCH /todolists/{todolistId}** – Update TodoList name
- **DELETE /todolists/{todolistId}** – Delete TodoList and items
- **PATCH /todolists/{todolistId}/todoitems/{todoitemId}** – Update TodoItem
- **DELETE /todolists/{todolistId}/todoitems/{todoitemId}** – Delete TodoItem

## Schema Details

**TodoList**
- `id` (string) – unique identifier
- `source_id` (string) – source system identifier
- `name` (string) – list name
- `created_at` (date-time) – creation timestamp
- `updated_at` (date-time) – last update timestamp
- `items` (array of TodoItem) – associated items

**TodoItem**
- `id` (string) – unique identifier
- `source_id` (string) – source system identifier
- `description` (string) – item description
- `completed` (boolean) – completion status
- `created_at` (date-time) – creation timestamp
- `updated_at` (date-time) – last update timestamp

**CreateTodoListBody**
- `source_id` (string) – required
- `name` (string) – required
- `items` (array of CreateTodoItemBody) – optional nested items

**CreateTodoItemBody**
- `source_id` (string)
- `description` (string)
- `completed` (boolean)

**UpdateTodoListBody**
- `name` (string) – updated list name

**UpdateTodoItemBody**
- `description` (string) – updated description
- `completed` (boolean) – updated status

**Note:** All IDs are strings (not integers). CreateTodoListBody supports nested item creation.

content.text:
Ya tengo una imagen clara de la consigna. Te explico antes de armar el plan, porque hay un par de decisiones que conviene que definas vos.

## Qué pide la Parte 2 (en criollo)

Tenés **dos sistemas con datos de Todos**:

1. **Tu app Rails** (la que hiciste en la entrevista) — guarda `TodoList` y `TodoItem` en su base local (SQLite).
2. **Una API externa** de Crunchloop (un servicio aparte, REST, base `http://localhost`, sin auth) que también guarda todolists e items.

El objetivo es **construir un mecanismo de sincronización** que mantenga ambos lados coordinados: que lo que se cree/edite/borre en un lado se refleje en el otro, soportando **create, update y delete**.

Un detalle clave que encontré en el OpenAPI (`docs/external-api.yaml`): los modelos de la API externa tienen un campo **`source_id`** además de su `id` propio (los ids externos son **strings**, no integers). Ese `source_id` es la pista de diseño central: está pensado para que vos mandes el id de **tu** sistema como "id de origen", y así puedas mapear cada registro local con su contraparte externa sin depender del id que genera la API externa.

Modelos externos relevantes:
- **TodoList**: `id` (string), `source_id` (string), `name`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `items[]`
- **TodoItem**: `id`, `source_id`, `description`, `completed`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `POST /todolists` acepta items anidados (podés crear la lista con sus items en **una sola** llamada → clave para "minimizar llamadas").

Ojo con el mapeo de campos local↔externo: tu `TodoItem` local usa `title`/`complete`, el externo usa `description`/`completed`.

## Qué hay que entregar

1. **Código de sincronización** (un servicio/módulo de sync) + **tests**.
2. **POC ejecutable** del sync (puede ser un rake task o script, incluso en el mismo archivo).
3. **`NOTES.md`** con: enfoque general, decisiones de diseño y trade-offs, estrategia de resiliencia/errores (fallos parciales + retries), edge cases, mejoras a futuro y supuestos.
4. Que todo **corra en dev containers** (ya tenés `.devcontainer/`).

Requisitos transversales que evalúan: **resiliencia** (fallos parciales, reintentos), **performance** (minimizar llamadas a la API externa), y **logging/errores claros**.

Antes de escribir el plan necesito que definas tres cosas:

questions.question: ¿Qué dirección de sincronización querés implementar?

options.label: One-way: Rails → externa (Recommended)
options.description: Tu app Rails es la fuente de verdad y empuja create/update/delete a la API externa. Más simple, encaja perfecto con el campo source_id, y alcanza para demostrar resiliencia y performance. Ideal para el alcance de una entrega de challenge.

options.label: Bidireccional (full)
options.description: Detectar cambios en ambos lados y resolver conflictos (last-write-wins, etc.). Más completo y se alinea con 'detectar cambios en la externa', pero mucho más complejo (necesita conflict resolution y tracking de cambios en los dos lados).

options.label: One-way ahora + diseño para bidi
options.description: Implementar push Rails→externa funcional, pero dejar la arquitectura y el NOTES.md preparados para extender a bidireccional. Buen balance entre entrega acotada y mostrar visión de diseño.

questions.question: ¿Cómo vamos a tener disponible la API externa para correr y testear?

options.label: Mockear/stubear en tests (Recommended)
options.description: El sync habla HTTP real, pero en los tests se stubea con WebMock/VCR. El POC apunta a una URL configurable por ENV. No depende de tener el servicio externo levantado. Lo más robusto para entregar.

options.label: Tengo un docker/imagen del servicio
options.description: Vos contás con una forma de levantar la API externa de Crunchloop (imagen docker, repo del servicio, etc.) y la integramos al docker-compose / devcontainer para correrla de verdad.

options.label: No sé cómo levantarla
options.description: No tenés acceso al servicio externo todavía; necesitás que investiguemos/preguntemos cómo obtenerlo antes de decidir.

questions.question: ¿Qué nivel de robustez en la ejecución del sync?

options.label: Rake task + retries en código (Recommended)
options.description: Servicio de sync invocable por un rake task (el POC), con manejo de fallos parciales y reintentos con backoff implementados en Ruby puro. Simple de correr y testear, sin infra extra.

options.label: Background jobs (ActiveJob/Sidekiq)
options.description: Encolar la sync en jobs con reintentos del framework. Más cercano a producción pero agrega dependencias (Redis/Sidekiq) y complejidad al devcontainer.

options.label: One-way: Rails → externa (Recommended)
options.description: Tu app Rails es la fuente de verdad y empuja create/update/delete a la API externa. Más simple, encaja perfecto con el campo source_id, y alcanza para demostrar resiliencia y performance. Ideal para el alcance de una entrega de challenge.

options.label: Bidireccional (full)
options.description: Detectar cambios en ambos lados y resolver conflictos (last-write-wins, etc.). Más completo y se alinea con 'detectar cambios en la externa', pero mucho más complejo (necesita conflict resolution y tracking de cambios en los dos lados).

options.label: One-way ahora + diseño para bidi
options.description: Implementar push Rails→externa funcional, pero dejar la arquitectura y el NOTES.md preparados para extender a bidireccional. Buen balance entre entrega acotada y mostrar visión de diseño.

questions.question: ¿Cómo vamos a tener disponible la API externa para correr y testear?

options.label: Mockear/stubear en tests (Recommended)
options.description: El sync habla HTTP real, pero en los tests se stubea con WebMock/VCR. El POC apunta a una URL configurable por ENV. No depende de tener el servicio externo levantado. Lo más robusto para entregar.

options.label: Tengo un docker/imagen del servicio
options.description: Vos contás con una forma de levantar la API externa de Crunchloop (imagen docker, repo del servicio, etc.) y la integramos al docker-compose / devcontainer para correrla de verdad.

options.label: No sé cómo levantarla
options.description: No tenés acceso al servicio externo todavía; necesitás que investiguemos/preguntemos cómo obtenerlo antes de decidir.

questions.question: ¿Qué nivel de robustez en la ejecución del sync?

options.label: Rake task + retries en código (Recommended)
options.description: Servicio de sync invocable por un rake task (el POC), con manejo de fallos parciales y reintentos con backoff implementados en Ruby puro. Simple de correr y testear, sin infra extra.

options.label: Background jobs (ActiveJob/Sidekiq)
options.description: Encolar la sync en jobs con reintentos del framework. Más cercano a producción pero agrega dependencias (Redis/Sidekiq) y complejidad al devcontainer.

answers.¿Qué nivel de robustez en la ejecución d: what about Solid Queue that it's the default now

content.text:
Dato importante que confirma el camino: el proyecto está en **Rails 7.0.4.3**, y **Solid Queue requiere Rails 7.1+** (en Rails 8 ya viene por default). Así que usar Solid Queue implica una actualización de Rails. Además noté que la tabla `todo_lists` **no tiene timestamps** (`created_at`/`updated_at`) — los vamos a necesitar para detectar cambios, así que habrá una migración sí o sí.

Sobre tu duda de dirección: la consigna efectivamente pide ambas cosas — *"detect changes in external API"* (crear local cuando aparece algo nuevo en la externa) **y** *"propagate local modifications"* (empujar lo local hacia afuera). Eso es una sincronización **bidireccional / de reconciliación**. Dejame confirmar dos cosas:

questions.question: Confirmemos el alcance de la sincronización (dada tu duda sobre crear registros locales desde la externa):

options.label: Bidireccional / reconciliación (Recommended)
options.description: Empuja create/update/delete de Rails→externa Y trae create/update/delete de externa→Rails (incluye 'crear TodoLists/TodoItems locales cuando aparecen en la externa'). Conflictos por last-write-wins usando updated_at. Cumple la consigna completa.

options.label: Solo pull externa→Rails
options.description: Enfocarse únicamente en traer/crear/actualizar/borrar registros locales según el estado de la API externa. Más acotado pero no cubre 'propagate local modifications'.

options.label: Solo push Rails→externa
options.description: Solo empujar cambios locales hacia la externa. No cubre 'crear locales cuando se detectan en la externa', que es justo tu duda.

questions.question: Solid Queue necesita actualizar Rails (hoy estás en 7.0.4.3). ¿Qué preferís para ejecutar/encolar el sync?

options.label: Rake task + ActiveJob (sin upgrade) (Recommended)
options.description: El sync es un service object (PORO) testeable, invocable por un rake task (el POC) y opcionalmente por un ActiveJob con retry_on. Retries con backoff en código. No toca la versión de Rails ni agrega infra al devcontainer. Más limpio para entregar.

options.label: Upgrade a Rails 7.1 + Solid Queue
options.description: Actualizar a 7.1 para sumar Solid Queue (durable, sin Redis). Más 'moderno' y cercano a prod, pero agrega upgrade de Rails + migración de tablas de la cola + proceso worker en el devcontainer.

options.label: Upgrade a Rails 8 + Solid Queue
options.description: Saltar a Rails 8 donde Solid Queue es el default. Lo más actual, pero el upgrade de 7.0→8 es el de mayor riesgo/esfuerzo y excede el foco del challenge (sync).
