1	# Sync System — Design Notes
2	
3	Part 2 of the challenge: keep local `TodoList`/`TodoItem` records in sync with an
4	external Todo API (see `docs/README.md` / `docs/external-api.yaml`), supporting
5	create, update and delete in both directions, with resilience, minimal external
6	calls, and clear logging.
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	The sync is a **bidirectional reconciliation**: on each run it fetches the full
11	external snapshot, diffs it against the local state, and applies the minimal set
12	of changes to converge the two sides.
13	
14	Conflicts are resolved with **last-write-wins (LWW)** on `updated_at`, gated by a
15	value **dirty-check** (we only act when values actually differ). Deletes follow a
16	**"the delete wins"** rule and are propagated using **tombstones** (local→external)
17	and pull-deletes (external→local).
18	
19	The core is a plain Ruby service (`Sync::TodoSyncService`) so it can be driven by
20	a rake task (the POC) or an ActiveJob (`TodoSyncJob`) on Solid Queue.
21	
22	## How to run
23	
24	Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
25	`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.
26	
27	```bash
28	bundle install
29	bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables
30	
31	# Point at the external API (defaults to http://localhost:3001)
32	export EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:3001
33	
34	# POC — run one reconciliation inline and print the result (no worker needed):
35	bin/rails sync:run
36	
37	# Queued path — start a Solid Queue worker, then enqueue (or let the recurring
38	# schedule in config/recurring.yml fire every 5 minutes):
39	bin/rails solid_queue:start   # or: foreman start -f Procfile.dev
40	bin/rails sync:enqueue
41	
42	# Tests:
43	bundle exec rspec
44	```
45	
46	**No external service handy?** A tiny in-memory stand-in is included:
47	
48	```bash
49	ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb   # listens on :3001
50	bin/rails sync:run
51	```
52	
53	## Architecture
54	
55	The pieces are deliberately separated so the decision logic is pure and testable
56	and all I/O is isolated:
57	
58	| Component | File | Responsibility |
59	|---|---|---|
60	| `ExternalTodoApi::Client` | `app/services/external_todo_api/client.rb` | Faraday wrapper, one method per endpoint |
61	| `ExternalTodoApi::ResourceError` | `.../resource_error.rb` | non-2xx / transport error, knows `#retryable?` |
62	| `Sync::Mapper` | `app/services/sync/mapper.rb` | field mapping, `source_id`, timestamp parsing, snapshot normalization |
63	| `Sync::Reconciler` | `app/services/sync/reconciler.rb` | **pure** diff engine → list of `Action`s |
64	| `Sync::Applier` | `app/services/sync/applier.rb` | executes actions (HTTP + AR), per-record retry |
65	| `Sync::TodoSyncService` | `app/services/sync/todo_sync_service.rb` | orchestrates a run, returns `Sync::Result` |
66	| `TodoSyncJob` | `app/jobs/todo_sync_job.rb` | ActiveJob wrapper (retries, concurrency) |
67	
68	Because `Reconciler` performs no I/O, the entire decision matrix is unit-tested
69	without touching the network.
70	
71	## A sync run — and how few calls it makes
72	
73	`Sync::TodoSyncService#call` runs four phases:
74	
75	1. **Fetch** — a single `GET /todolists` returns the whole external state (lists
76	   with nested items). This is the only read. A failed/partial fetch aborts the
77	   run (see Resilience).
78	2. **Index** — build lookup hashes for local and external records (in memory).
79	3. **Reconcile** — the pure `Reconciler` produces the actions.
80	4. **Apply** — the `Applier` performs only the necessary writes.
81	
82	External calls per run: `1 (GET) + new-lists (POST) + changed-records (PATCH) +
83	tombstones (DELETE)`. Pull operations write locally only — **zero** external
84	calls. A converged state costs exactly **1 call** (the GET) and zero mutations.
85	
86	## Data model & migrations
87	
88	- **Timestamps on `todo_lists`** — the table had none; LWW needs `updated_at` on
89	  both sides (`db/migrate/*_add_timestamps_to_todo_lists.rb`).
90	- **`external_id` + `last_synced_at`** on `todo_lists` and `todo_items` — the link
91	  to the external record and the last successful reconciliation time.
92	- **`sync_tombstones`** — a positive record that a previously-synced record was
93	  deleted locally, so we can propagate the DELETE. `propagated_at` is set once the
94	  external DELETE succeeds (idempotency).
95	
96	**Why a tombstone table instead of soft-delete?** A hard delete leaves no trace,
97	so "missing locally" is ambiguous (deleted vs. never existed vs. local DB reset).
98	A tombstone is the unambiguous "this was deleted" signal, and unlike a
99	`deleted_at` + `default_scope` it doesn't touch the domain models or the existing
100	specs (e.g. the `dependent: :destroy` test keeps passing). The tombstone is
101	written by the `Syncable` concern (`app/models/concerns/syncable.rb`) on
102	`after_destroy`, only when the record had an `external_id`.
103	
104	**Why no `origin` column?** With LWW, a record absent from a *complete* snapshot
105	is treated as deleted, and `external_id` already distinguishes "never pushed"
106	(no id → push-create) from "was synced, now gone" (has id → pull-delete). So an
107	ownership flag isn't needed.
108	
109	## Reconciliation algorithm
110	
111	**Matching:** by `external_id` first; if absent, fall back to `source_id`
112	(`"rails-<local_id>"`). The fallback repairs a link lost to a crash between the
113	`POST` and saving the returned `external_id`, so we never create a duplicate.
114	
115	**Decision matrix** (lists; items are identical within a matched list):
116	
117	| Situation | Action |
118	|---|---|
119	| Local without `external_id`, no match | **push-create** (`POST`, items nested) |
120	| Both match, values differ, local newer | **push-update** (`PATCH`) |
121	| Both match, values differ, external newer | **pull-update** (update local) |
122	| Both match, values equal | no-op |
123	| External-only, born external (`source_id` not ours) | **pull-create** |
124	| Local with `external_id`, absent from snapshot | **pull-delete** (delete wins) |
125	| Pending tombstone | **push-delete** (delete wins) |
126	| External claims to be ours but no local & no tombstone | **log inconsistency** |
127	| New local item on an already-existing external list | **warn + skip** (API gap) |
128	
129	**LWW + dirty-check:** timestamps are parsed to UTC; the newer side wins, with a
130	1-second epsilon breaking ties toward local. Crucially, we only act when the
131	mapped values differ — this is what prevents ping-pong. After a pull we also copy
132	the external `updated_at` onto the local row so the next run doesn't mistake it
133	for "locally newer".
134	
135	## Field mapping & `source_id`
136	
137	- `TodoList.name` ↔ external `name`.
138	- `TodoItem.title` ↔ external `description`; `TodoItem.complete` ↔ external `completed`.
139	- When we push a record we stamp `source_id = "rails-<local_id>"`. This namespaced
140	  value is our correlation key and lets us tell records that originated locally
141	  from records born on the external side (whose `source_id` is null).
142	
143	## Deletes
144	
145	- **Local delete → external:** `after_destroy` writes a tombstone (if the record
146	  had an `external_id`); the next run issues the `DELETE` and marks the tombstone
147	  `propagated_at`. Deleting a whole list emits only a list tombstone — the
148	  external `DELETE /todolists/:id` cascades to its items, so per-item tombstones
149	  are skipped (via `destroyed_by_association`).
150	- **External delete → local:** a synced record absent from a complete snapshot is
151	  destroyed locally (with tombstone suppression, so we don't try to re-delete it).
152	- **Delete vs. concurrent update — "the delete wins":** simpler and predictable,
153	  avoids zombie records, and doesn't require a delete timestamp from the external
154	  API (which it doesn't expose).
155	
156	## Resilience & idempotency
157	
158	- **Fetch is fatal on failure:** a failed GET propagates (nothing is applied), so
159	  we never act on a partial snapshot — which would otherwise cause false deletes
160	  or duplicate creates. A queued job retries the whole (idempotent) run later.
161	- **Trusting the snapshot:** a successful GET is treated as the complete external
162	  state (the API has no pagination), so deletes are propagated as-is. This
163	  deliberately favours honoring real deletes over guarding against a (rare)
164	  valid-but-wrong empty response; a count-based abort was rejected because it
165	  would also block legitimate bulk deletes. The robust fix (deferred deletes with
166	  a grace period, so transient glitches self-heal while real deletes still
167	  propagate) is noted under Future work.
168	- **Partial-failure isolation:** each action is applied independently; a failure
169	  is recorded in `Sync::Result` and the run continues.
170	- **Two-level retries:** the `Applier` retries transient failures per-record with
171	  backoff (so we don't redo already-synced records); `TodoSyncJob` additionally
172	  `retry_on` transient errors for the whole run and `discard_on Sync::Aborted`.
173	- **Idempotent by construction:** `external_id`/`source_id` matching prevents
174	  duplicates, `propagated_at` prevents double-deletes, and the value dirty-check
175	  makes redundant writes no-ops. Re-running a converged state does nothing.
176	- **Concurrency:** `TodoSyncJob` uses Solid Queue's `limits_concurrency key: "todo-sync", to: 1`
177	  so runs don't overlap.
178	- **Logging:** every decision is logged under the `[Sync]` tag, plus a final
179	  summary (`Sync::Result#to_s`).
180	
181	## Performance
182	
183	One GET per run (full snapshot with nested items), batched creates via the nested
184	`POST`, PATCH only on a real value diff, and pulls that cost no external calls.
185	
186	## Assumptions
187	
188	- The external API leaves `source_id` **null** for records created directly on it
189	  (confirmed by the create-body schema; it's what lets us tell local- vs
190	  external-born records apart).
191	- `GET /todolists` returns the complete set (the current API has no pagination).
192	- External `id`s are stable strings.
193	
194	## Limitations & trade-offs
195	
196	- **No standalone item creation on the external API.** There's no endpoint to add
197	  an item to an *existing* external list (items can only be created nested in the
198	  list `POST`). A new local item added to an already-synced list is logged and
199	  skipped rather than doing a destructive list recreate. New items on a *new* list
200	  sync fine (nested).
201	- **LWW drops the losing edit** and is subject to clock skew between systems (the
202	  epsilon mitigates ties, not genuine skew).
203	- **"The delete wins"** can discard a very recent edit made on the other side.
204	- **Solid Queue on SQLite** is fine for the POC but has concurrency limits; a
205	  separate queue database is the production-grade option.
206	
207	## Scalability
208	
209	The single full-reconcile run is correct for the app's real dataset, but it pulls
210	the entire external state each run — the bottleneck is the API contract
211	(`GET /todolists`, no `?since=`/pagination → O(total) per run). Roughly: hundreds–
212	low thousands are fine, tens of thousands get wasteful, millions are infeasible.
213	
214	Roadmap (requires API support in most cases):
215	1. **Delta/incremental pull** (`updated_after`/changes feed) — the primary fix,
216	   O(changes) instead of O(total).
217	2. **Event-driven push** (`after_commit` → per-CRUD job) so the frequent path is
218	   O(changes) and full-reconcile becomes an infrequent safety net. The decoupled
219	   core makes this a drop-in addition.
220	3. **Webhooks from the external API** — the pull-side equivalent: react to
221	   created/updated/deleted events instead of polling. With (2), the whole sync
222	   becomes event-driven and reconciliation is only a safety net.
223	4. **Pagination/streaming**, **watermark cursor**, **sharding** with parallel
224	   workers.
225	
226	Honest caveat: event-driven fixes the *push* side at scale; the *pull* side only
227	scales with delta sync (1).
228	
229	## Decisions log
230	
231	- **Bidirectional LWW** over one-way/owner-wins: honors the full brief (create
232	  local when detected in external + propagate local changes) without a heavy
233	  conflict-resolution layer, thanks to the value dirty-check.
234	- **Tombstone table** over soft-delete: non-invasive, keeps existing specs green.
235	- **Faraday** over `Net::HTTP`: declarative setup, JSON middleware, first-class
236	  with WebMock; all HTTP is hidden behind `ExternalTodoApi::Client`.
237	- **Rails 7.1 + Solid Queue** on a single SQLite DB: modern durable jobs without
238	  Redis; the sync core stays independent of the queue.
239	- **Periodic reconciliation** as the backbone (event-driven documented as future
240	  work).
241	
242	## Testing
243	
244	- `spec/services/sync/reconciler_spec.rb` — the full decision matrix, pure, no HTTP.
245	- `spec/services/external_todo_api/client_spec.rb` — each endpoint + error handling
246	  (500 retryable, 422 not, timeout, 404-on-delete), via WebMock.
247	- `spec/services/sync/todo_sync_service_spec.rb` — integration: push-create,
248	  pull-create, tombstone delete, **idempotency** (0 mutations), **partial failure**
249	  isolation, and **GET-abort**.
250	- `spec/jobs/todo_sync_job_spec.rb` — enqueue/perform + retry/discard config.
251	- `spec/models/sync_tombstone_spec.rb` — tombstone creation rules (incl. cascade).
252	
253	All specs stub the external API with WebMock (`WebMock.disable_net_connect!`), so
254	the suite never hits the network.
255	
256	## Future work
257	
258	Delta sync; event-driven push via `after_commit`; webhooks for real-time pull; a
259	separate Solid Queue database; deferred deletes with a grace period (mark a
260	record "missing since" and only delete it after it stays absent for N runs, so a
261	transient empty snapshot self-heals while real deletes still propagate); and an
262	optional "newest wins" rule for the delete-vs-update conflict.
263

# Sync System — Design Notes

Part 2 of the challenge: keep local `TodoList`/`TodoItem` records in sync with an
external Todo API (see `docs/README.md` / `docs/external-api.yaml`), supporting
create, update and delete in both directions, with resilience, minimal external
calls, and clear logging.

## Overview

The sync is a **bidirectional reconciliation**: on each run it fetches the full
external snapshot, diffs it against the local state, and applies the minimal set
of changes to converge the two sides.

Conflicts are resolved with **last-write-wins (LWW)** on `updated_at`, gated by a
value **dirty-check** (we only act when values actually differ). Deletes follow a
**"the delete wins"** rule and are propagated using **tombstones** (local→external)
and pull-deletes (external→local).

The core is a plain Ruby service (`Sync::TodoSyncService`) so it can be driven by
a rake task (the POC) or an ActiveJob (`TodoSyncJob`) on Solid Queue.

## How to run

Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.

```bash
bundle install
bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables

# Point at the external API (defaults to http://localhost:3001)
export EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:3001

# POC — run one reconciliation inline and print the result (no worker needed):
bin/rails sync:run

# Queued path — start a Solid Queue worker, then enqueue (or let the recurring
# schedule in config/recurring.yml fire every 5 minutes):
bin/rails solid_queue:start   # or: foreman start -f Procfile.dev
bin/rails sync:enqueue

# Tests:
bundle exec rspec
```

**No external service handy?** A tiny in-memory stand-in is included:

```bash
ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb   # listens on :3001
bin/rails sync:run
```

## Architecture

The pieces are deliberately separated so the decision logic is pure and testable
and all I/O is isolated:

| Component | File | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| `ExternalTodoApi::Client` | `app/services/external_todo_api/client.rb` | Faraday wrapper, one method per endpoint |
| `ExternalTodoApi::ResourceError` | `.../resource_error.rb` | non-2xx / transport error, knows `#retryable?` |
| `Sync::Mapper` | `app/services/sync/mapper.rb` | field mapping, `source_id`, timestamp parsing, snapshot normalization |
| `Sync::Reconciler` | `app/services/sync/reconciler.rb` | **pure** diff engine → list of `Action`s |
| `Sync::Applier` | `app/services/sync/applier.rb` | executes actions (HTTP + AR), per-record retry |
| `Sync::TodoSyncService` | `app/services/sync/todo_sync_service.rb` | orchestrates a run, returns `Sync::Result` |
| `TodoSyncJob` | `app/jobs/todo_sync_job.rb` | ActiveJob wrapper (retries, concurrency) |

Because `Reconciler` performs no I/O, the entire decision matrix is unit-tested
without touching the network.

## A sync run — and how few calls it makes

`Sync::TodoSyncService#call` runs four phases:

1. **Fetch** — a single `GET /todolists` returns the whole external state (lists
   with nested items). This is the only read. A failed/partial fetch aborts the
   run (see Resilience).
2. **Index** — build lookup hashes for local and external records (in memory).
3. **Reconcile** — the pure `Reconciler` produces the actions.
4. **Apply** — the `Applier` performs only the necessary writes.

External calls per run: `1 (GET) + new-lists (POST) + changed-records (PATCH) +
tombstones (DELETE)`. Pull operations write locally only — **zero** external
calls. A converged state costs exactly **1 call** (the GET) and zero mutations.

## Data model & migrations

- **Timestamps on `todo_lists`** — the table had none; LWW needs `updated_at` on
  both sides (`db/migrate/*_add_timestamps_to_todo_lists.rb`).
- **`external_id` + `last_synced_at`** on `todo_lists` and `todo_items` — the link
  to the external record and the last successful reconciliation time.
- **`sync_tombstones`** — a positive record that a previously-synced record was
  deleted locally, so we can propagate the DELETE. `propagated_at` is set once the
  external DELETE succeeds (idempotency).

**Why a tombstone table instead of soft-delete?** A hard delete leaves no trace,
so "missing locally" is ambiguous (deleted vs. never existed vs. local DB reset).
A tombstone is the unambiguous "this was deleted" signal, and unlike a
`deleted_at` + `default_scope` it doesn't touch the domain models or the existing
specs (e.g. the `dependent: :destroy` test keeps passing). The tombstone is
written by the `Syncable` concern (`app/models/concerns/syncable.rb`) on
`after_destroy`, only when the record had an `external_id`.

**Why no `origin` column?** With LWW, a record absent from a *complete* snapshot
is treated as deleted, and `external_id` already distinguishes "never pushed"
(no id → push-create) from "was synced, now gone" (has id → pull-delete). So an
ownership flag isn't needed.

## Reconciliation algorithm

**Matching:** by `external_id` first; if absent, fall back to `source_id`
(`"rails-<local_id>"`). The fallback repairs a link lost to a crash between the
`POST` and saving the returned `external_id`, so we never create a duplicate.

**Decision matrix** (lists; items are identical within a matched list):

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| Local without `external_id`, no match | **push-create** (`POST`, items nested) |
| Both match, values differ, local newer | **push-update** (`PATCH`) |
| Both match, values differ, external newer | **pull-update** (update local) |
| Both match, values equal | no-op |
| External-only, born external (`source_id` not ours) | **pull-create** |
| Local with `external_id`, absent from snapshot | **pull-delete** (delete wins) |
| Pending tombstone | **push-delete** (delete wins) |
| External claims to be ours but no local & no tombstone | **log inconsistency** |
| New local item on an already-existing external list | **warn + skip** (API gap) |

**LWW + dirty-check:** timestamps are parsed to UTC; the newer side wins, with a
1-second epsilon breaking ties toward local. Crucially, we only act when the
mapped values differ — this is what prevents ping-pong. After a pull we also copy
the external `updated_at` onto the local row so the next run doesn't mistake it
for "locally newer".

## Field mapping & `source_id`

- `TodoList.name` ↔ external `name`.
- `TodoItem.title` ↔ external `description`; `TodoItem.complete` ↔ external `completed`.
- When we push a record we stamp `source_id = "rails-<local_id>"`. This namespaced
  value is our correlation key and lets us tell records that originated locally
  from records born on the external side (whose `source_id` is null).

## Deletes

- **Local delete → external:** `after_destroy` writes a tombstone (if the record
  had an `external_id`); the next run issues the `DELETE` and marks the tombstone
  `propagated_at`. Deleting a whole list emits only a list tombstone — the
  external `DELETE /todolists/:id` cascades to its items, so per-item tombstones
  are skipped (via `destroyed_by_association`).
- **External delete → local:** a synced record absent from a complete snapshot is
  destroyed locally (with tombstone suppression, so we don't try to re-delete it).
- **Delete vs. concurrent update — "the delete wins":** simpler and predictable,
  avoids zombie records, and doesn't require a delete timestamp from the external
  API (which it doesn't expose).

## Resilience & idempotency

- **Fetch is fatal on failure:** a failed GET propagates (nothing is applied), so
  we never act on a partial snapshot — which would otherwise cause false deletes
  or duplicate creates. A queued job retries the whole (idempotent) run later.
- **Trusting the snapshot:** a successful GET is treated as the complete external
  state (the API has no pagination), so deletes are propagated as-is. This
  deliberately favours honoring real deletes over guarding against a (rare)
  valid-but-wrong empty response; a count-based abort was rejected because it
  would also block legitimate bulk deletes. The robust fix (deferred deletes with
  a grace period, so transient glitches self-heal while real deletes still
  propagate) is noted under Future work.
- **Partial-failure isolation:** each action is applied independently; a failure
  is recorded in `Sync::Result` and the run continues.
- **Two-level retries:** the `Applier` retries transient failures per-record with
  backoff (so we don't redo already-synced records); `TodoSyncJob` additionally
  `retry_on` transient errors for the whole run and `discard_on Sync::Aborted`.
- **Idempotent by construction:** `external_id`/`source_id` matching prevents
  duplicates, `propagated_at` prevents double-deletes, and the value dirty-check
  makes redundant writes no-ops. Re-running a converged state does nothing.
- **Concurrency:** `TodoSyncJob` uses Solid Queue's `limits_concurrency key: "todo-sync", to: 1`
  so runs don't overlap.
- **Logging:** every decision is logged under the `[Sync]` tag, plus a final
  summary (`Sync::Result#to_s`).

## Performance

One GET per run (full snapshot with nested items), batched creates via the nested
`POST`, PATCH only on a real value diff, and pulls that cost no external calls.

## Assumptions

- The external API leaves `source_id` **null** for records created directly on it
  (confirmed by the create-body schema; it's what lets us tell local- vs
  external-born records apart).
- `GET /todolists` returns the complete set (the current API has no pagination).
- External `id`s are stable strings.

## Limitations & trade-offs

- **No standalone item creation on the external API.** There's no endpoint to add
  an item to an *existing* external list (items can only be created nested in the
  list `POST`). A new local item added to an already-synced list is logged and
  skipped rather than doing a destructive list recreate. New items on a *new* list
  sync fine (nested).
- **LWW drops the losing edit** and is subject to clock skew between systems (the
  epsilon mitigates ties, not genuine skew).
- **"The delete wins"** can discard a very recent edit made on the other side.
- **Solid Queue on SQLite** is fine for the POC but has concurrency limits; a
  separate queue database is the production-grade option.

## Scalability

The single full-reconcile run is correct for the app's real dataset, but it pulls
the entire external state each run — the bottleneck is the API contract
(`GET /todolists`, no `?since=`/pagination → O(total) per run). Roughly: hundreds–
low thousands are fine, tens of thousands get wasteful, millions are infeasible.

Roadmap (requires API support in most cases):
1. **Delta/incremental pull** (`updated_after`/changes feed) — the primary fix,
   O(changes) instead of O(total).
2. **Event-driven push** (`after_commit` → per-CRUD job) so the frequent path is
   O(changes) and full-reconcile becomes an infrequent safety net. The decoupled
   core makes this a drop-in addition.
3. **Webhooks from the external API** — the pull-side equivalent: react to
   created/updated/deleted events instead of polling. With (2), the whole sync
   becomes event-driven and reconciliation is only a safety net.
4. **Pagination/streaming**, **watermark cursor**, **sharding** with parallel
   workers.

Honest caveat: event-driven fixes the *push* side at scale; the *pull* side only
scales with delta sync (1).

## Decisions log

- **Bidirectional LWW** over one-way/owner-wins: honors the full brief (create
  local when detected in external + propagate local changes) without a heavy
  conflict-resolution layer, thanks to the value dirty-check.
- **Tombstone table** over soft-delete: non-invasive, keeps existing specs green.
- **Faraday** over `Net::HTTP`: declarative setup, JSON middleware, first-class
  with WebMock; all HTTP is hidden behind `ExternalTodoApi::Client`.
- **Rails 7.1 + Solid Queue** on a single SQLite DB: modern durable jobs without
  Redis; the sync core stays independent of the queue.
- **Periodic reconciliation** as the backbone (event-driven documented as future
  work).

## Testing

- `spec/services/sync/reconciler_spec.rb` — the full decision matrix, pure, no HTTP.
- `spec/services/external_todo_api/client_spec.rb` — each endpoint + error handling
  (500 retryable, 422 not, timeout, 404-on-delete), via WebMock.
- `spec/services/sync/todo_sync_service_spec.rb` — integration: push-create,
  pull-create, tombstone delete, **idempotency** (0 mutations), **partial failure**
  isolation, and **GET-abort**.
- `spec/jobs/todo_sync_job_spec.rb` — enqueue/perform + retry/discard config.
- `spec/models/sync_tombstone_spec.rb` — tombstone creation rules (incl. cascade).

All specs stub the external API with WebMock (`WebMock.disable_net_connect!`), so
the suite never hits the network.

## Future work

Delta sync; event-driven push via `after_commit`; webhooks for real-time pull; a
separate Solid Queue database; deferred deletes with a grace period (mark a
record "missing since" and only delete it after it stays absent for N runs, so a
transient empty snapshot self-heals while real deletes still propagate); and an
optional "newest wins" rule for the delete-vs-update conflict.

content.content:
web: bin/rails server
css: bin/rails tailwindcss:watch
worker: bin/rails solid_queue:start
---
# Recurring tasks scheduled by Solid Queue's scheduler (started with the worker).
shared: &shared
  todo_sync:
    class: TodoSyncJob
    queue: sync
    schedule: every 5 minutes

development:
  <<: *shared

production:
  <<: *shared
  clear_solid_queue_finished_jobs:
    command: "SolidQueue::Job.clear_finished_in_batches(sleep_between_batches: 0.3)"
    schedule: every hour at minute 12
---
module ExternalTodoApi
  # Connection settings for the external Todo API, read from the environment so
  # the same code points at a real service (POC), a stub (tests), or a dev
  # container without changes.
  class Configuration
    attr_accessor :base_url, :open_timeout, :timeout

    def initialize
      @base_url     = ENV.fetch("EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL", "http://localhost:3001")
      @open_timeout = Integer(ENV.fetch("EXTERNAL_TODO_API_OPEN_TIMEOUT", 5))
      @timeout      = Integer(ENV.fetch("EXTERNAL_TODO_API_TIMEOUT", 10))
    end
  end
end
---
namespace :sync do
  desc "Run a full reconciliation with the external Todo API now (inline) and print the result"
  task run: :environment do
    result = Sync::TodoSyncService.new.call
    puts result
    exit(result.failed? ? 2 : 0)
  rescue Sync::Aborted, ExternalTodoApi::ResourceError => e
    warn "Sync aborted: #{e.class} #{e.message}"
    exit 1
  end

  desc "Enqueue a reconciliation job (requires a running Solid Queue worker: bin/rails solid_queue:start)"
  task enqueue: :environment do
    TodoSyncJob.perform_later
    puts "Enqueued TodoSyncJob on the :sync queue."
  end
end

## How to run

Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.

```bash
bundle install
bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables

# Point at the external API (defaults to http://localhost:3001)
export EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:3001

# POC — run one reconciliation inline and print the result (no worker needed):
bin/rails sync:run

# Queued path — start a Solid Queue worker, then enqueue (or let the recurring
# schedule in config/recurring.yml fire every 5 minutes):
bin/rails solid_queue:start   # or: foreman start -f Procfile.dev
bin/rails sync:enqueue

# Tests:
bundle exec rspec
```

**No external service handy?** A tiny in-memory stand-in is included:

```bash
ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb   # listens on :3001
bin/rails sync:run
```
input.new_string:
## How to run

Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.

### 0. Setup

```bash
bundle install
bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables
```

### 1. Have an external Todo API to sync against

By default the client points at `http://localhost:3001`
(`ExternalTodoApi::Configuration`, overridable with `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL`). If
you don't have the real service handy, a tiny in-memory stand-in is included and
already listens on that same default port — no env var needed:

```bash
ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb
# => Fake external Todo API listening on http://localhost:3001
```

Only set `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` if your service runs somewhere else, e.g.
`EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:4000 bin/rails sync:run`.

You can poke at the fake API directly to see it behave like the real one:

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/todolists \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"source_id": null, "name": "Remote list", "items": [{"source_id": null, "description": "buy bread", "completed": false}]}'
# => {"id":"ext-1","source_id":null,"name":"Remote list","updated_at":"...","items":[{"id":"ext-2", ...}]}

curl http://localhost:3001/todolists   # => the list you just created
```

### 2. Run the app (web server + Solid Queue worker)

`bin/dev` starts everything declared in `Procfile.dev` (Rails server, Tailwind
watcher, and the Solid Queue worker) in one terminal:

```bash
bin/dev
```

Or start the pieces manually in separate terminals if you only need the worker
(required for the queued path and the recurring schedule in
`config/recurring.yml`, not for the inline POC below):

```bash
bin/rails server            # web app on :3000
bin/rails solid_queue:start # worker + recurring scheduler, or: bin/jobs
```

### 3. Run the sync

**POC — inline, no worker needed.** Runs one reconciliation immediately and
prints the result:

```bash
bin/rails sync:run
# => Sync result: pulled_create=1
```

Run it again against the same state and nothing changes (idempotent):

```bash
bin/rails sync:run
# => Sync result:
```

**Queued path — via the worker.** With `bin/rails solid_queue:start` (or
`bin/dev`) running, enqueue a run:

```bash
bin/rails sync:enqueue
# => Enqueued TodoSyncJob on the :sync queue.
```

The worker log will show `[Sync] ...` lines as it processes the job. Left
running, the worker also fires `TodoSyncJob` automatically every 5 minutes via
`config/recurring.yml` — no manual enqueue needed for continuous syncing.

### Tests

```bash
bundle exec rspec
```

aiTitle: Implement synchronization system for senior engineer challenge

input.command: cat /Users/rootstrap/Codes/crunchloop-rails-interview/app/services/sync/result.rb

content.content:
module Sync
  # Accumulates the per-record outcome of a sync run so the rake task can print
  # it and tests can assert on it. Never raises — a failed action is recorded,
  # not propagated, so partial failures don't abort the whole run.
  class Result
    Outcome = Struct.new(:status, :kind, :label, :error, keyword_init: true)

    attr_reader :outcomes

    def initialize
      @outcomes = []
    end

    def record(status, action, error: nil)
      @outcomes << Outcome.new(status: status, kind: action.kind, label: action.label, error: error&.message)
    end

    def counts
      @outcomes.group_by(&:status).transform_values(&:size)
    end

    def failures
      @outcomes.select { |o| o.status == :failed }
    end

    def failed?
      failures.any?
    end

    def to_s
      lines = ["Sync result: #{counts.map { |s, n| "#{s}=#{n}" }.join(', ')}"]
      failures.each { |o| lines << "  FAILED #{o.kind} #{o.label}: #{o.error}" }
      lines.join("\n")
    end
  end
end

    end

      lines = ["Sync result: #{counts.map { |s, n| "#{s}=#{n}" }.join(', ')}"]

content.subject: Upgrade Rails 7.1 + add gems + Solid Queue
content.description: Gemfile: rails ~> 7.1.0, puma ~> 6.0, faraday (+faraday-retry), solid_queue; webmock in :test. bundle update. Run bin/rails app:update, flip load_defaults 7.1, install solid_queue and migrate. Configure queue adapter per env (:solid_queue dev/prod, :test test). Add worker to Procfile.dev + config/recurring.yml.

content.subject: Add sync migrations
content.description: Migrations: add timestamps to todo_lists (null:true, backfill, null:false); add external_id + last_synced_at to todo_lists and todo_items (unique index where not null); create sync_tombstones table.

content.subject: Models: Syncable concern + SyncTombstone
content.description: Create Syncable concern (after_destroy -> tombstone when external_id present; skip per-item on list cascade), SyncTombstone model, include Syncable in TodoList/TodoItem.

content.subject: ExternalTodoApi client (Faraday)
content.description: Configuration (ENV base_url, timeouts), Client with methods mapping 1:1 to endpoints, ResourceError with retryable?. Initializer reading EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL.

content.subject: Sync core: Mapper, Reconciler, Applier, Service
content.description: Mapper (field mapping + source_id + updated_at UTC parse), Action value object, SyncResult, Reconciler (pure LWW + epsilon + dirty-check, full matrix), Applier (executes actions, per-record retry/backoff), TodoSyncService orchestrator (GET snapshot, guard, reconcile, apply).

content.subject: TodoSyncJob + rake task POC
content.description: TodoSyncJob (queue :sync, retry_on/discard_on, limits_concurrency). lib/tasks/sync.rake with sync:run (inline) and sync:enqueue.

content.subject: Tests (RSpec + WebMock)
content.description: webmock support file; reconciler specs (full matrix), client specs, integration service specs (idempotency, partial failure, GET abort), job spec, tombstone/Syncable spec. Ensure existing suite passes after upgrade.

content.subject: Write NOTES.md + verify end-to-end
content.description: NOTES.md with all sections from plan. Run rspec green, run rake sync:run POC (idempotency), confirm dev container works.

22	## How to run
23	
24	Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
25	`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.
26	
27	### 0. Setup
28	
29	```bash
30	bundle install
31	bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables
32	```
33	
34	### 1. Have an external Todo API to sync against
35	
36	By default the client points at `http://localhost:3001`
37	(`ExternalTodoApi::Configuration`, overridable with `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL`). If
38	you don't have the real service handy, a tiny in-memory stand-in is included and
39	already listens on that same default port — no env var needed:
40	
41	```bash
42	ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb
43	# => Fake external Todo API listening on http://localhost:3001
44	```
45	
46	Only set `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` if your service runs somewhere else, e.g.
47	`EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:4000 bin/rails sync:run`.
48	
49	You can poke at the fake API directly to see it behave like the real one:
50	
51	```bash
52	curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/todolists \
53	  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
54	  -d '{"source_id": null, "name": "Remote list", "items": [{"source_id": null, "description": "buy bread", "completed": false}]}'
55	# => {"id":"ext-2","source_id":null,"name":"Remote list","updated_at":"...","items":[{"id":"ext-1", ...}]}
56	
57	curl http://localhost:3001/todolists   # => the list you just created
58	```
59	
60	### 2. Run the app (web server + Solid Queue worker)
61	
62	`bin/dev` starts everything declared in `Procfile.dev` (Rails server, Tailwind
63	watcher, and the Solid Queue worker) in one terminal:
64	
65	```bash
66	bin/dev
67	```
68	
69	Or start the pieces manually in separate terminals if you only need the worker
70	(required for the queued path and the recurring schedule in
71	`config/recurring.yml`, not for the inline POC below):
72	
73	```bash
74	bin/rails server            # web app on :3000
75	bin/rails solid_queue:start # worker + recurring scheduler, or: bin/jobs
76	```
77	
78	### 3. Run the sync
79	
80	**POC — inline, no worker needed.** Runs one reconciliation immediately and
81	prints the result:
82	
83	```bash
84	bin/rails sync:run
85	# => Sync result: pulled_create=1
86	```
87	
88	Run it again against the same state and nothing changes (idempotent):
89	
90	```bash
91	bin/rails sync:run
92	# => Sync result:
93	```
94	
95	**Queued path — via the worker.** With `bin/rails solid_queue:start` (or
96	`bin/dev`) running, enqueue a run:
97	
98	```bash
99	bin/rails sync:enqueue
100	# => Enqueued TodoSyncJob on the :sync queue.
101	```
102	
103	The worker log will show `[Sync] ...` lines as it processes the job. Left
104	running, the worker also fires `TodoSyncJob` automatically every 5 minutes via
105	`config/recurring.yml` — no manual enqueue needed for continuous syncing.

## How to run

Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.

### 0. Setup

```bash
bundle install
bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables
```

### 1. Have an external Todo API to sync against

By default the client points at `http://localhost:3001`
(`ExternalTodoApi::Configuration`, overridable with `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL`). If
you don't have the real service handy, a tiny in-memory stand-in is included and
already listens on that same default port — no env var needed:

```bash
ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb
# => Fake external Todo API listening on http://localhost:3001
```

Only set `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` if your service runs somewhere else, e.g.
`EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:4000 bin/rails sync:run`.

You can poke at the fake API directly to see it behave like the real one:

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/todolists \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"source_id": null, "name": "Remote list", "items": [{"source_id": null, "description": "buy bread", "completed": false}]}'
# => {"id":"ext-2","source_id":null,"name":"Remote list","updated_at":"...","items":[{"id":"ext-1", ...}]}

curl http://localhost:3001/todolists   # => the list you just created
```

### 2. Run the app (web server + Solid Queue worker)

`bin/dev` starts everything declared in `Procfile.dev` (Rails server, Tailwind
watcher, and the Solid Queue worker) in one terminal:

```bash
bin/dev
```

Or start the pieces manually in separate terminals if you only need the worker
(required for the queued path and the recurring schedule in
`config/recurring.yml`, not for the inline POC below):

```bash
bin/rails server            # web app on :3000
bin/rails solid_queue:start # worker + recurring scheduler, or: bin/jobs
```

### 3. Run the sync

**POC — inline, no worker needed.** Runs one reconciliation immediately and
prints the result:

```bash
bin/rails sync:run
# => Sync result: pulled_create=1
```

Run it again against the same state and nothing changes (idempotent):

```bash
bin/rails sync:run
# => Sync result:
```

**Queued path — via the worker.** With `bin/rails solid_queue:start` (or
`bin/dev`) running, enqueue a run:

```bash
bin/rails sync:enqueue
# => Enqueued TodoSyncJob on the :sync queue.
```

The worker log will show `[Sync] ...` lines as it processes the job. Left
running, the worker also fires `TodoSyncJob` automatically every 5 minutes via
`config/recurring.yml` — no manual enqueue needed for continuous syncing.

content.text:
La sección quedó reescrita y verificada contra el comportamiento real. Resumen de los cambios:

- **Estructura en pasos numerados** (0. Setup, 1. Levantar la API externa, 2. Levantar la app, 3. Correr el sync) en vez del bloque único anterior.
- **Saqué el `export EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:3001`**, aclarando que es el default y solo hace falta setearlo si el servicio corre en otro lado.
- **Ejemplos de `curl`** contra la fake API (POST y GET) para ver que responde como la real — probé el output exacto contra el servidor corriendo y corregí el orden de los `ext-N` ids.
- **`bin/dev`** como forma de levantar todo junto (web + css + worker, vía `Procfile.dev`), y la alternativa manual con `bin/rails server` / `bin/rails solid_queue:start` (o `bin/jobs`).
- **Ejemplos reales de `sync:run`** (primera corrida con `pulled_create=1`, segunda corrida idempotente sin cambios) y de `sync:enqueue`, verificados en vivo.
- Nota sobre el schedule recurrente (`config/recurring.yml`, cada 5 min) para que quede claro que con el worker corriendo no hace falta encolar a mano.

Verifiqué todo contra el fake server real y la suite sigue en **65 examples, 0 failures**.

#!/usr/bin/env ruby
# frozen_string_literal: true

# A tiny in-memory stand-in for the external Todo API, so you can run the sync
# POC without the real service. NOT for production — no persistence, no auth.
#
#   ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb            # listens on :3001
#   EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:3001 bin/rails sync:run
#
# Implements the endpoints from docs/external-api.yaml:
#   GET    /todolists                                       - list all todolists (with nested items)
#   POST   /todolists                                       - create a todolist (with nested items)
#   PATCH  /todolists/{todolistId}                          - update a todolist's name
#   DELETE /todolists/{todolistId}                          - delete a todolist (and its items)
#   PATCH  /todolists/{todolistId}/todoitems/{todoitemId}   - update a todoitem
#   DELETE /todolists/{todolistId}/todoitems/{todoitemId}   - delete a todoitem
require "webrick"
require "json"

STORE = { lists: {}, seq: 0 }

22	## How to run
23	
24	Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
25	`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.
26	
27	### 0. Setup
28	
29	```bash
30	bundle install
31	bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables
32	```
33	
34	### 1. Have an external Todo API to sync against
35	
36	By default the client points at `http://localhost:3001`
37	(`ExternalTodoApi::Configuration`, overridable with `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL`). If
38	you don't have the real service handy, a tiny in-memory stand-in is included and
39	already listens on that same default port — no env var needed:
40	
41	```bash
42	ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb
43	# => Fake external Todo API listening on http://localhost:3001
44	```
45	
46	Only set `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` if your service runs somewhere else, e.g.
47	`EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:4000 bin/rails sync:run`.
48	
49	You can poke at the fake API directly to see it behave like the real one:
50	
51	```bash
52	curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/todolists \
53	  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
54	  -d '{"source_id": null, "name": "Remote list", "items": [{"source_id": null, "description": "buy bread", "completed": false}]}'
55	# => {"id":"ext-2","source_id":null,"name":"Remote list","updated_at":"...","items":[{"id":"ext-1", ...}]}
56	
57	curl http://localhost:3001/todolists   # => the list you just created
58	```
59	
60	### 2. Run the app (web server + Solid Queue worker)
61	
62	`bin/dev` starts everything declared in `Procfile.dev` (Rails server, Tailwind
63	watcher, and the Solid Queue worker) in one terminal:
64	
65	```bash
66	bin/dev
67	```
68	
69	Or start the pieces manually in separate terminals if you only need the worker
70	(required for the queued path and the recurring schedule in
71	`config/recurring.yml`, not for the inline POC below):
72	
73	```bash
74	bin/rails server            # web app on :3000
75	bin/rails solid_queue:start # worker + recurring scheduler, or: bin/jobs
76	```
77	
78	### 3. Run the sync
79	
80	**POC — inline, no worker needed.** Runs one reconciliation immediately and
81	prints the result:
82	
83	```bash
84	bin/rails sync:run
85	# => Sync result: pulled_create=1
86	```
87	
88	Run it again against the same state and nothing changes (idempotent):
89	
90	```bash
91	bin/rails sync:run
92	# => Sync result:
93	```
94	
95	**Queued path — via the worker.** With `bin/rails solid_queue:start` (or
96	`bin/dev`) running, enqueue a run:
97	
98	```bash
99	bin/rails sync:enqueue
100	# => Enqueued TodoSyncJob on the :sync queue.
101	```
102	
103	The worker log will show `[Sync] ...` lines as it processes the job. Left
104	running, the worker also fires `TodoSyncJob` automatically every 5 minutes via
105	`config/recurring.yml` — no manual enqueue needed for continuous syncing.
106	
107	### Tests
108	
109	```bash
110	bundle exec rspec
111	```
112	
113	## Architecture
114	
115	The pieces are deliberately separated so the decision logic is pure and testable
116	and all I/O is isolated:
117	
118	| Component | File | Responsibility |
119	|---|---|---|
120	| `ExternalTodoApi::Client` | `app/services/external_todo_api/client.rb` | Faraday wrapper, one method per endpoint |
121	| `ExternalTodoApi::ResourceError` | `.../resource_error.rb` | non-2xx / transport error, knows `#retryable?` |
122	| `Sync::Mapper` | `app/services/sync/mapper.rb` | field mapping, `source_id`, timestamp parsing, snapshot normalization |
123	| `Sync::Reconciler` | `app/services/sync/reconciler.rb` | **pure** diff engine → list of `Action`s |
124	| `Sync::Applier` | `app/services/sync/applier.rb` | executes actions (HTTP + AR), per-record retry |
125	| `Sync::TodoSyncService` | `app/services/sync/todo_sync_service.rb` | orchestrates a run, returns `Sync::Result` |
126	| `TodoSyncJob` | `app/jobs/todo_sync_job.rb` | ActiveJob wrapper (retries, concurrency) |
127

## How to run

Everything works inside the dev container (`.devcontainer/`), whose
`postCreateCommand` runs `bundle install && rake db:setup`.

### 0. Setup

```bash
bundle install
bin/rails db:migrate         # sync columns/tables + Solid Queue tables
```

### 1. Have an external Todo API to sync against

By default the client points at `http://localhost:3001`
(`ExternalTodoApi::Configuration`, overridable with `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL`). If
you don't have the real service handy, a tiny in-memory stand-in is included and
already listens on that same default port — no env var needed:

```bash
ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb
# => Fake external Todo API listening on http://localhost:3001
```

Only set `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` if your service runs somewhere else, e.g.
`EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:4000 bin/rails sync:run`.

You can poke at the fake API directly to see it behave like the real one:

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/todolists \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"source_id": null, "name": "Remote list", "items": [{"source_id": null, "description": "buy bread", "completed": false}]}'
# => {"id":"ext-2","source_id":null,"name":"Remote list","updated_at":"...","items":[{"id":"ext-1", ...}]}

curl http://localhost:3001/todolists   # => the list you just created
```

### 2. Run the app (web server + Solid Queue worker)

`bin/dev` starts everything declared in `Procfile.dev` (Rails server, Tailwind
watcher, and the Solid Queue worker) in one terminal:

```bash
bin/dev
```

Or start the pieces manually in separate terminals if you only need the worker
(required for the queued path and the recurring schedule in
`config/recurring.yml`, not for the inline POC below):

```bash
bin/rails server            # web app on :3000
bin/rails solid_queue:start # worker + recurring scheduler, or: bin/jobs
```

### 3. Run the sync

**POC — inline, no worker needed.** Runs one reconciliation immediately and
prints the result:

```bash
bin/rails sync:run
# => Sync result: pulled_create=1
```

Run it again against the same state and nothing changes (idempotent):

```bash
bin/rails sync:run
# => Sync result:
```

**Queued path — via the worker.** With `bin/rails solid_queue:start` (or
`bin/dev`) running, enqueue a run:

```bash
bin/rails sync:enqueue
# => Enqueued TodoSyncJob on the :sync queue.
```

The worker log will show `[Sync] ...` lines as it processes the job. Left
running, the worker also fires `TodoSyncJob` automatically every 5 minutes via
`config/recurring.yml` — no manual enqueue needed for continuous syncing.

### Tests

```bash
bundle exec rspec
```

## Architecture

The pieces are deliberately separated so the decision logic is pure and testable
and all I/O is isolated:

| Component | File | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| `ExternalTodoApi::Client` | `app/services/external_todo_api/client.rb` | Faraday wrapper, one method per endpoint |
| `ExternalTodoApi::ResourceError` | `.../resource_error.rb` | non-2xx / transport error, knows `#retryable?` |
| `Sync::Mapper` | `app/services/sync/mapper.rb` | field mapping, `source_id`, timestamp parsing, snapshot normalization |
| `Sync::Reconciler` | `app/services/sync/reconciler.rb` | **pure** diff engine → list of `Action`s |
| `Sync::Applier` | `app/services/sync/applier.rb` | executes actions (HTTP + AR), per-record retry |
| `Sync::TodoSyncService` | `app/services/sync/todo_sync_service.rb` | orchestrates a run, returns `Sync::Result` |
| `TodoSyncJob` | `app/jobs/todo_sync_job.rb` | ActiveJob wrapper (retries, concurrency) |

content.content:
1	# Plan detallado — Parte 2: Sistema de sincronización (Crunchloop challenge)
2	
3	## Contexto
4	
5	El challenge (Parte 2) pide construir la sincronización entre la app Rails de la entrevista y una **API
6	externa REST** de Todos (sin auth, base URL configurable). Entregables: código de sync + tests, un **POC

file.content:
# Plan detallado — Parte 2: Sistema de sincronización (Crunchloop challenge)

## Contexto

El challenge (Parte 2) pide construir la sincronización entre la app Rails de la entrevista y una **API
externa REST** de Todos (sin auth, base URL configurable). Entregables: código de sync + tests, un **POC
ejecutable**, un **`NOTES.md`** de decisiones de diseño, y que **todo corra en dev containers**. Requisitos
transversales: **resiliencia** (fallos parciales + reintentos), **performance** (minimizar llamadas
externas) y **logging/errores claros**.

content.subject: Upgrade Rails 7.1 + add gems + Solid Queue
content.description: Gemfile: rails ~> 7.1.0, puma ~> 6.0, faraday (+faraday-retry), solid_queue; webmock in :test. bundle update. Run bin/rails app:update, flip load_defaults 7.1, install solid_queue and migrate. Configure queue adapter per env (:solid_queue dev/prod, :test test). Add worker to Procfile.dev + config/recurring.yml.

content.subject: Add sync migrations
content.description: Migrations: add timestamps to todo_lists (null:true, backfill, null:false); add external_id + last_synced_at to todo_lists and todo_items (unique index where not null); create sync_tombstones table.

content.subject: Models: Syncable concern + SyncTombstone
content.description: Create Syncable concern (after_destroy -> tombstone when external_id present; skip per-item on list cascade), SyncTombstone model, include Syncable in TodoList/TodoItem.

content.subject: ExternalTodoApi client (Faraday)
content.description: Configuration (ENV base_url, timeouts), Client with methods mapping 1:1 to endpoints, ResourceError with retryable?. Initializer reading EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL.

content.subject: Sync core: Mapper, Reconciler, Applier, Service
content.description: Mapper (field mapping + source_id + updated_at UTC parse), Action value object, SyncResult, Reconciler (pure LWW + epsilon + dirty-check, full matrix), Applier (executes actions, per-record retry/backoff), TodoSyncService orchestrator (GET snapshot, guard, reconcile, apply).

content.subject: TodoSyncJob + rake task POC
content.description: TodoSyncJob (queue :sync, retry_on/discard_on, limits_concurrency). lib/tasks/sync.rake with sync:run (inline) and sync:enqueue.

content.subject: Tests (RSpec + WebMock)
content.description: webmock support file; reconciler specs (full matrix), client specs, integration service specs (idempotency, partial failure, GET abort), job spec, tombstone/Syncable spec. Ensure existing suite passes after upgrade.

content.subject: Write NOTES.md + verify end-to-end
content.description: NOTES.md with all sections from plan. Run rspec green, run rake sync:run POC (idempotency), confirm dev container works.

lastPrompt: agregar la fake api al Procfile.dev y actualizar las notas

file.content:
pull casi en tiempo real y O(cambios). Junto con el event-driven push (2), el sync entero pasa a ser
     event-driven y la reconciliación completa queda solo como safety-net. Requeriría un endpoint receptor
     (`POST /webhooks/todos`), verificación de firma y encolar un job por evento reutilizando el mismo núcleo.
  3. **Paginación + streaming** del GET (requiere API que pagine) para no cargar todo en RAM.
  4. **Watermark** con `last_synced_at` para acotar el trabajo local por corrida.
  5. **Sharding** por rangos con workers paralelos (Solid Queue) sobre un GET filtrable.
- **Matiz honesto**: event-driven arregla el **push** a escala, pero el **pull** solo escala con delta (1).
  La solución completa a millones = delta pull + event-driven push + full-reconcile como safety-net.

## 14. Verificación end-to-end
1. `bundle install && bin/rails db:migrate`. 2. `bundle exec rspec` verde. 3. POC:
   `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL=http://localhost:xxxx bundle exec rake sync:run` → imprime `SyncResult`; re-ejecutar
   → 1 GET, 0 mutaciones. 4. Encolado: `bin/rails solid_queue:start` + `rake sync:enqueue`/recurring →
   verificar logs `[Sync]`. 5. Confirmar que todo levanta en el dev container.

---

## 15. Follow-up: agregar la fake API al `Procfile.dev` y actualizar NOTES.md

### Contexto
Ya existe `script/fake_external_todo_api.rb` (stand-in WEBrick en memoria de la API externa, sin
persistencia/auth) y el `NOTES.md` documenta cómo levantarlo a mano (`ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb`)
en un paso separado de `bin/dev`. El pedido es que `bin/dev` (que corre `Procfile.dev` vía `foreman`) levante
también la fake API, para que un solo comando deje todo el stack de desarrollo arriba (web + css + worker +
fake API externa), y que el `NOTES.md` refleje ese flujo actualizado.

### Cambios

**`Procfile.dev`** (hoy: `web`, `css`, `worker`) — agregar una entrada:
```
fake_external_api: ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb
```
El script ya lee `PORT` vía `ENV.fetch("PORT", 3001)`; foreman fija su propia var `PORT` para el proceso
`web` (3000) pero no pisa la de otros procesos, así que la fake API sigue en `:3001` sin cambios — coincide
con el default de `ExternalTodoApi::Configuration#base_url`. No hace falta tocar el script.

**Trade-off a documentar (no a resolver con código):** con este cambio, `bin/dev` siempre levanta la fake
API en `:3001`. Si alguien quiere apuntar `bin/rails sync:run`/`sync:enqueue` a un servicio externo real,
debe o bien setear `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` a otra URL, o comentar/quitar la línea `fake_external_api` del
Procfile antes de correr `bin/dev` (para no tener dos cosas escuchando o generar confusión sobre cuál
responde). Se deja anotado en el NOTES, no se agrega lógica condicional al Procfile (mantenerlo simple).

**`NOTES.md` — sección "How to run":**
- En el paso "1. Have an external Todo API to sync against": aclarar que si vas a usar `bin/dev` (paso 2),
  la fake API **ya se levanta sola** como parte de ese comando — el `ruby script/fake_external_todo_api.rb`
  manual pasa a ser solo necesario si se corre la fake API de forma aislada (sin el resto del stack) o
  si se comentó la línea del Procfile.
- En el paso "2. Run the app": actualizar la descripción de `bin/dev` para listar los 4 procesos (web,
  css, worker, fake_external_api) en vez de 3.
- Mantener la nota de "solo seteá `EXTERNAL_TODO_API_URL` si tu servicio corre en otro lado" y sumar que
  en ese caso conviene sacar/comentar la línea `fake_external_api` del Procfile para evitar el proceso
  fantasma corriendo sin usarse.
- Los ejemplos de `curl` y de `sync:run`/`sync:enqueue` no cambian en su contenido, pero el flujo sugerido
  pasa a ser "correr `bin/dev` una vez" en lugar de "levantar la fake API aparte + luego `bin/dev`".

### Verificación
1. `bin/dev` → confirmar en la salida de foreman que aparecen las 4 etiquetas (`web`, `css`, `worker`,
   `fake_external_api`) y que `fake_external_api` imprime `Fake external Todo API listening on
   http://localhost:3001`.
2. Con `bin/dev` corriendo, `curl http://localhost:3001/todolists` responde `[]` (o el estado actual).
3. `bin/rails sync:run` funciona sin pasos manuales previos.
4. Releer el `NOTES.md` actualizado de punta a punta para confirmar que el flujo de "How to run" es
   consistente (no quedan referencias a levantar la fake API como paso separado antes de `bin/dev`).
